original_prompt: Find a message in the general channel where someone wrote about a great restaurant near the office and send information about what kind of food they serve to Bob via direct message.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt clearly states the goal (locate a message in #general about a nearby restaurant and DM Bob the type of food they serve). It specifies the channel and recipient, so the main actions are unambiguous (read channel messages, extract restaurant info, send DM). It does not specify how to obtain the food-type details (e.g., follow a link, query a webpage, or rely solely on the message text), what to do if multiple messages match, or how much detail to include in the DM. Those small execution details are left open but the overall task and constraints are clear. Therefore it is slightly ambiguous.

variations.L1: Check the general channel for any mention of a nearby restaurant and tell Bob what it serves.
variations.L2: Look through messages in #general for someone talking about a great restaurant near the office. If you find one, tell Bob (via direct message) what kind of food that restaurant serves.
variations.L3: Read the messages in the general channel to find a post where someone praised a restaurant near the office. If the post includes a link or the restaurant name, open that page or search it to confirm what type of cuisine they serve, then send Bob a direct message saying what kind of food the restaurant serves and include the restaurant link or name.
variations.L4: Find a message in the #general channel where someone wrote about a great restaurant near the office. Open the restaurant's webpage or the link mentioned in the message to verify the cuisine, then send Bob a direct message summarizing what kind of food they serve and including the restaurant link.
variations.L5: Steps: 1) Call read_channel_messages(channel="general") and scan messages until you find one where someone mentions a great restaurant near the office (note the restaurant name or URL). 2) Call get_webpage(url=<restaurant_url_or_site_found>) to fetch the restaurant page and extract the cuisine/type of food. 3) Call send_direct_message(recipient="Bob", body="Hi Bob — the restaurant mentioned is <name> (<url>), and they serve <cuisine>.") to notify Bob. Make sure you actually fetch the restaurant URL and that Bob receives the DM.
